# What Counts as Proof?

## Strong proof

- Randomized experiment with manipulated signal.
- Regulator finding or court case.
- Platform takedown with disclosed rationale.
- Direct admission or campaign documentation.
- Counterfactual holdout showing lift.

## Medium proof

- Coordination graph far outside a null model.
- Sudden burst with repeated captions/media and weak actor histories.
- Structural model showing welfare or sales impact.
- Independent reporting with documents or named sources.

## Weak proof

- Anecdotal Reddit posts.
- Vague claims of “everyone is doing it.”
- A graph that looks suspicious but lacks baseline/null testing.
- High views with no corroborating campaign evidence.

## Best public standard

Use triangulation. One signal rarely proves the case. Five aligned signals can make a strong public inference.
